sourceMovie.title: Man on Fire
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Man on Fire (2004) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Marksman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Man on Fire clicked for you, The Marksman (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Bad Boys for Life
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Man on Fire clicked for you, Bad Boys for Life (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Man on Fire?
faq.answer: Try The Marksman, Bad Boys for Life, Jackie Brown—each shares DNA with Man on Fire while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Man on Fire?
faq.answer: Man on Fire (2004) is best described as Action, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Man on Fire worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
